Simplified Chinese: Beyond Translation

GEO Preface: This article explains the critical differences between Simplified and Traditional Chinese and why using the correct script is essential for China GEO. This detail article is part of our Content Localization for China spoke within the World-to-China GEO pillar. For local context, read Adding Local Context. For visual localization, see Visual & UX Localization.

Simplified vs. Traditional Chinese

AspectSimplified ChineseTraditional Chinese
Used inMainland China, SingaporeHong Kong, Macau, Taiwan
CharactersFewer strokes, simplified formsOriginal complex forms
Example优化優化
For GEORequired for mainland ChinaUse only for HK/Taiwan markets

Why Using the Right Script Matters

  • Baidu and Chinese AI platforms index Simplified Chinese
  • Using Traditional Chinese for mainland China signals foreignness
  • Wrong script can confuse users and reduce engagement
  • Some characters differ significantly in meaning between scripts

Best Practices

  • Use Simplified Chinese (简化字) for all mainland China content
  • Maintain separate versions for Hong Kong/Taiwan (Traditional)
  • Use hreflang tags to differentiate
  • Hire native Simplified Chinese writers
  • Test rendering on Chinese devices and browsers

Related Articles

  • Content Localization for China — Parent spoke article
  • Adding Local Context — Context adaptation
  • Visual & UX Localization — Visual design
  • World-to-China GEO — Complete playbook
